prompt:
Feature ticket for our Java ontap-checker tool. Today the tool only does a cluster probe (ClusterProbe -> GET /api/cluster) and that part works fine — keep it working. What ops actually asked for is a 'who am I really' check: since ONTAP 9.19.1 there's a proper whoami endpoint that tells you the username, roles and permissions your credentials resolve to, and we want the checker to use it. But half our fleet is still on older ONTAP, so the probe has to degrade gracefully: on an older cluster fall back to the cluster version endpoint and report that whoami isn't supported there — that is NOT an error. What IS an error: a 403 (our service account lacks rights — I want a distinct exception type so the runbook can tell 'no permission' from 'old cluster'), and a 401. One more thing from the security review: someone keeps asking for an --insecure switch; the answer is no — the client builder must expose insecureTls() that refuses (throws) with a message naming TLS, so the request stops coming up.

Research this first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to confirm the CURRENT official NetApp documentation for the whoami capability — at minimum the ONTAP REST API what's-new page (which release introduced whoami), the whoami resource reference (NetApp's official client-library docs on library.netapp.com document the exact endpoint path and response fields), and the GET /api/cluster reference for the fallback fields. Only first-party NetApp documentation (docs.netapp.com, NetApp's official NetAppDocs docs source on GitHub, or library.netapp.com) counts as a source. Compare what you find against the pinned contract in docs/contract.json (provenance in docs/official_sources.json) — both protected like TestMain.java; if current docs contradict the pinned contract, report the conflict in your summary instead of editing protected files. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final response.

Acceptance is `java TestMain.java` (TestMain compiles the whole directory). It expects, alongside the existing classes: class IdentityProbe with a constructor taking OntapRestClient and a probe() method returning IdentityReport; class IdentityReport with public fields whoamiSupported (boolean), username (String), roles (List<String>), privileges (List of IdentityReport.Privilege with public String fields path and access), clusterName (String), versionFull (String), and Integer generation/major/minor (null unless the fallback ran); class OntapPermissionException extending OntapApiException; and the insecureTls() builder method described above. The whoami request must ask for explicit fields (username,roles,privileges), the fallback must ask for fields=name,version, roles/privileges must come back as empty lists (never null) on the fallback path, and the 403/401 paths must not issue any fallback request. Don't touch TestMain.java or anything under docs/. Run the acceptance command when you're done and cite the official URLs you used in your final message.

research.product: NetApp ONTAP REST API — whoami identity endpoint (new in ONTAP 9.19.1)
research.interface: GET /api/security/login/whoami with GET /api/cluster version fallback for pre-9.19.1 clusters

official_sources.title: netapp_ontap.resources.whoami — NetApp ONTAP Python client library API documentation (library.netapp.com)
official_sources.used_for: exact endpoint GET /api/security/login/whoami and its response shape: username (string), roles (list of role names), privileges (list of {path, access}, e.g. {"path": "/api", "access": "all"}), plus _links.self

official_sources.title: What's new with the ONTAP REST API (NetApp official docs source for docs.netapp.com/us-en/ontap-automation/whats-new.html)
official_sources.used_for: the whoami capability ('retrieve the username, role, and permissions information for the logged in user') is new in ONTAP 9.19.1 — older clusters do not serve it

official_sources.title: Security resources in the ONTAP REST API (NetApp official docs source)
official_sources.used_for: confirmation that the whoami resource displays the authenticated user's username, role, and permissions and was introduced with ONTAP 9.19.1

official_sources.title: Retrieve the cluster configuration (GET /api/cluster)
official_sources.used_for: fallback probe fields: name and the version object {full, generation, major, minor} used to report identity capability on pre-9.19.1 clusters

official_sources.title: Getting started with the ONTAP REST API (NetApp official docs source)
official_sources.used_for: base path /api, HTTP basic authentication, fields query parameter, and the {error:{code,message,target}} error object used to distinguish 401/403/404 responses

research.language_rationale: Java is where our fleet tooling lives, and ONTAP's documented integration surface for it is the raw REST API (NetApp's own clients are generated from the same OpenAPI contract). java.net.http.HttpClient exercises the documented surface hermetically with zero dependencies, and Java's default TLS validation is exactly the behavior this probe must never disable.
